questions.question: In digital circuit design, which of the following is a common abstract representation of a combinational logic function that can be implemented using a lookup table (LUT) or gates?
In a typical Von Neumann architecture, which stage of the instruction pipeline is responsible for interpreting the operation code (opcode) and identifying the operands required for an instruction?

In modern System-on-Chip (SoC) designs, particularly for embedded systems with tight latency requirements, what is the primary benefit of using "Scratchpad Memory" (SPM) over traditional cache memory for certain critical data or instructions?
options: Automatic cache coherence across multiple processors.
Guarantees deterministic access latency as it is software-managed.
Offers higher capacity than traditional DRAM modules.
Reduces dynamic power consumption by completely eliminating static power.
Simplifies memory management unit (MMU) design by removing virtual addressing.

questions.question: In advanced semiconductor manufacturing, particularly at sub-10nm technology nodes, which phenomenon describes the statistical variation in physical dimensions and electrical characteristics of nominally identical devices on a single die, primarily due to inherent randomness in the lithographic and etching processes?

Beyond logic equivalence checking, what is the primary advantage of employing "Formal Verification" techniques, such as Model Checking, in complex digital IC design flows compared to traditional simulation-based verification?
options: It can execute testbenches much faster than cycle-accurate simulators.
It can exhaustively prove the absence of certain design errors across all possible inputs and states.
It automatically generates optimal test vectors for functional coverage.
It provides detailed waveforms that are easier for designers to debug.
It is primarily used for verifying analog mixed-signal interfaces.
